course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG6480-01 Psychiatric Practicum 1  (Carol Anne Marchetti)
comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["I have never had an instructor that I have grown to respect so much. I'm not one to strongly agree with everything but Dr Marchetti deserves it. The strengths of this course includes going back over and linking information from past courses and telling us which course and where in that past course the information could be found. I liked that we were encouraged to bring our tough cases to class and share them with the class. This allowed us to share openly. It scared me at first, the getting vulnerable with my peers. It became so comforting that I looked forward to it. We have been in class together for two years, slowly getting to know each other. These are my peers, my now friends, and my future colleagues. I did an online Bachelor's program and I knew not oneI did like using Exxat, even though it drove me nuts at first because it took me a minute to get used to using it but once I did, it was easy keeping up with the documentation while I was in clinical. This made it easier to address with my preceptor while I was present in clinical. I really noticed my retention of the different med combinations and which medication belonged in which category and preferred starting points as well as contraindications started sticking in my brain. Dr Marchetti is truly a gifted instructor and I am so very grateful I will be able to work with her next semester."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG6480-01 Psychiatric Practicum 1  (Carol Anne Marchetti)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["She should continue with what she is doing because she is awesome!"]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG6480-01 Psychiatric Practicum 1  (Carol Anne Marchetti)
comment_prompt: Please expand on the instructor’s strengths and/or areas for improvement in facilitating inclusive learning.
comments_json: ["The only issue that I have with this class had nothing to do with Dr Marchetti at all. It had to do with a portion of the class that we were told was going to be part of the curriculum and this was the Fitzgerald review course. Prior to the first day of this summer semester, we had a meeting in which we were told what to expect, we were told that we would be starting board prep on week 10 of this semester, week 10 of the fall semester and then just prior to our boards. It was built into our modules and when week 10 rolled around and I asked about it, we were told that Fitzgerald was a suggestion and was NOT part of the modules or the program. If we want the review course, we have to pay for it privately and study on our own. I was disappointed that it was misrepresented and I thought it seemed like a gross move on Northeastern's part. Moving forward, I think that if Northeastern is not planning on following through with something so important, then they shouldn't make promises they don't plan on keeping."]

This would also perhaps help to facilitate a more intimate group discussion within the breakout room.", "Like I mentioned above, more breakout rooms.", "Nothing. Dr. Van Pelt is a pleasure to learn from and how she incorporates wellness time with challenging topics and is always free to meet with students makes her a truly great professor for all of us", "Less group projects.", "Manage the course with the co–professor, the Dr Van Pelt brings a wealth of knowledge as well as a personality, communication skills and understanding that when coupled enhance learning. Especially in the virtual setting when we are so disconnected and our virtual interactions are very meaningful", "This is overall, not just for Dr. VP; I struggled to learn a lot from the power points. I personally felt they were hard to follow at times, due to the density of the information. For example, the frameworks were hard to keep up with and the concept maps of different types of study designs were equally challenging to follow. I do understand this is building block class but I think a primary resource to utilize that will help us conceptualize the DNP project is needed for students to stay grounded and to connect the pieces of the overall project.", "Some of the assignments in this course felt like excessive busy work. For example: the evidence appraisal table assignment. I felt like cutting this assignment's requirements in half would still achieve the same learning experience, and possibly allow for more individualized feedback for each student's work from the instructors.", "none", "I think that it would be beneficial to have more time to brainstorm DNP project ideas during class time with classmates in breakout rooms. We did some brainstorming as a class, however, it was usually one or two people talking in front of the entire class.  Perhaps creating breakout rooms categorized by topic of interest (i.e. patient safety focused, Women's health, global health etc.) would be useful for students to think of ideas together."]

comment_prompt: Please expand on the instructor’s strengths and/or areas for improvement in facilitating inclusive learning.
comments_json: ["Clear and concise rubric for course assignments.", "All strengths, no weaknesses!", "NA", "I appreciated the positivity and encouragement from this instructor.", "Maybe occasionally calling on students to open up discussion.  Sometimes on zoom it is hard to know if someone else is going to start talking at the same time, so I think everyone pauses and then the chance is over.", "I found Dr. Van Pelt extremely kind and passionate regarding not only the course content, but maintaining student wellness. There were a few classes where we were given time to brainstorm potential DNP project topics which was really helpful, not only to hear other student ideas, but for professors to weigh in on potential project ideas.", "N/a", "I loved Dr. van Pelt's wellness techniques at the beginning of her lectures.", "see above", "Appreciated the wellness time at the beginning of class.", "Dr. VanPelt is a wonderful and knowledgeable instructor. I found her to be incredibly approachable and really valued the detailed constructive feedback she provided on assignments. I learned a lot from her recommendations and appreciated the insight she provided. I felt like she actively listened to all students during their presentations and provided great feedback and questions to consider. I really enjoyed the mindfulness moments that she incorporated into every class and hope she continues with them in future courses.", "This course was well designed and I felt it was taught by two experts in the topic area. The main area for improvement was the approach to student presentations. Both professors abruptly cut off student presentations multiple times during the semester. These interactions felt disrespectful and caused a lot of second hand embarrassment as well as anxiety amongst the students. This anxiety seemed to limit student–student engagement after presentations and limited overall opportunities for learning. I do not believe either professor intended to be disrespectful or had poor intentions, but do believe steps can be taken to prevent similar interactions in the future. The major underlying issue appeared to come from presentation expectations. This is a class of high achieving students and that manifested in a number of unnecessarily detailed and lengthy student presentations. Additionally, many of the students likely haven't presented in years given our career field. At the beginning of the semester and in the written assignments it should be made overly clear that presentations are a means of conveying concise, yet effective information and sticking to the allotted time limits is extremely important for attention and out of respect for other students who need to present. This should be briefly reiterated at the start of each presentation day. Having preset start times for each student presentation on the last day seemed helpful in keeping students on track, but this also might have been from ample time for practice and a better understanding of expectations at that point. Alternatively, I think splitting the class presentation in two breakout rooms should be considered in the future. It would be unfortunate to not hear everyone's presentations, but as the class size gets bigger each breakout room will probably be close to the same size as one class 5–10 years ago. All that being said, I do think some presentation discipline is necessary to help students become better and more effective presenters. It just may require a little clearer expectations and gentler reinforcement in the future.", "Dr. Van Pelt is an excellent instructor and an amazing resource. She's thoughtful with her feedback and is very encouraging to all students.", "I enjoyed being able to learn about research and developing PICOT questions and appraising research, but have limited pressure to choose a DNP project topic, but allowing us to explore the research space. I think this laid a very good foundation for future research courses and DNP project.", "Dr. Van Pelt was very approachable and made herself easily reachable outside of class to answer questions.", "I really like Dr. Van Pelt's transparency. Shes not afraid to let you know where / how you can perform better and where you may have come up short in an assignment, but she does it in a way that promotes increased performance and increased understanding.", "Very inclusive!!", "Felt very inclusive at all times", "I think the assignments were quite large in scope and instructions. I feel like the objectives for the class could've been met with assignments that built off of the concepts slower. The evidence synthesis assignment was so broad to connect, it sort of made my head spin at times and I almost felt more confused by the project as I went on. I think I may feel more anxious about the DNP project now than I was before taking the research course.", "none"]

Some of these opinions may change drastically by the end of our education as we gain more CRNA specific skills and feel increasingly confident in ourselves, but I do think there were muted opinions from many students and just having the alternative point of view discussion might help with learning on these topics further and prepare us to have difficult discussion with politicians, physician anesthesiologists, AAs and the general public. This is a very loaded topic that requires extensive future research to build on a lot of the strong data already provided by the AANA and non–partial sources, but I think just discussing the alternative viewpoints in class will do a lot to facilitate students learning and help us come to our own conclusions. comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["I felt that the instructors did a poor job of separating the expectations of this class from those of our practicum course. I often felt like Dr. Hall spoke about concepts and practicum as if they were one course, which made it confusing to navigate at times.  Nonetheless, I admire both the instructors and their willingness to share their expertise. They also do a fantastic job creating a non–judgmental classroom space. I just think they could be more organized.", "last minute uploads of course content, sometimes with less than 24 hours to review prior to class,  inconsistency with being told things could be a variety of ways irl, but come exam time there is a right and wrong way to do something.", "It went smoothly", "Great", "I felt the organization of modules was kind of confusing and overwhelming, especially because it was a \"sister\" class to the practicum but the material didn't always coincide and wasn't always consistent. I think in the future it should be organized a bit better, and also I think some of the material could have been incorporated into previous courses so it wasn't as overwhelming.", "I love the format of the course where we can watch lectures ahead of time and then apply the concepts in class. It is a valuable use of class time to have discussion and apply what we read and listened to.", "Online course organization was difficult to follow at times, but I was able to manage.", "I found the structure of this class challenging. The lectures were at times not uploaded until much later than when covered, mainly the peer lectures. Also the time of the class changed frequently without an announcement being sent out, and I had a hard time knowing when to show up. I wish we broke into different groups each week when reviewing care plans, because I felt like I did not interact with most of my cohort between being in the same groups in concepts, and lab groups in practicum. I also wish that the professors required different speakers each week, it often seemed like the same people spoke for their group each time, and it left some feeling like they didnt have a voice in their group, and others like they were getting it dumped on them every time.", "This course was well suited for an online structure. Canvas organization was a little confusing at first, there seemed to be a lot of content that we wasn't applicable to our class until much later in the semester.", "I enjoyed having the recorded lecture to watch beforehand and then using class as a time to review, ask questions, and practice questions. However, there were weeks when the prelecture was not posted until 1–2 days prior and it would have beneficial if they were uploaded on Thursdays.", "I found the pre–recorded lectures helpful", "there were a few miscommunications about start times for a few weeks", "It was very effective for my learning"]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Connie Lorette)
comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["This is a great course. I love simulation and the opportunity to learn from these professors. However, there was more than once occasions where we got less than 24 hours' notice that we were expected to attend a Zoom class. I think these easily could have been planned out at the beginning of the semester. I know I have heard professors say that we should expect to have school commitments Monday–Wednesday, but it makes it challenging if I have a meeting for my DNP project planned during that time, or something else related to school.", "poor communication, for example, the peer quiz had no information on what would be on it or when the expected time was to take and submit, assignments are graded without even having a rubric for clear expectations on what should be included in the submission", "The course schedule was a little difficult to navigate.", "Went smoothly", ".", "This course was sometimes confusing in its structure....lectures with Dr. Lorette were sometimes announced with very little notice, and expectations were unclear in terms of knowledge level.", "Organization of online content for the course was difficult to navigate at times.", "This course was mostly in–person", "This course's online component was very helpful in preparing me for in person simulation.", "Every instructor was great throughout this course. I think there are a few organizational aspects that could be improved, such as the TBA class Zooms and the TBA peer quiz. I found it difficult to adequately prepare for class when the additional Zoom classes were announced only the day before. This made it challenging to schedule our personal and school commitments without knowing when and what time the extra Zooms would be more than 24 hours in advance. I also could have prepared better for the peer quiz if a date had been specified for its release, but it was opened at the last minute with little prior notice.Regarding SIM, it was nice to finally practice what we've discussed all summer. I think having some form of \"open lab hours\" outside of our scheduled SIM time would be helpful to familiarize ourselves more with the machine and go through the induction/emergence sequence. I also felt quite flustered during the final practice SIM with the addition of the surgeon, especially when they were asking for things while I was laser–focused on completing the anesthesia sequence in the correct order. I understand that this might be similar to real life, but perhaps it would be beneficial to introduce the surgeon and related aspects earlier in the SIMs. Overall, I learned a lot from every professor this semester, and I am excited about the amount I still have to learn.", "I found this class's format challenging. I did not have issues with the peer lectures, as we were notified via the syllabus. But we often did not know if we were meeting for mini zooms with Dr. Lorette until less than 24 hours before. I understand we are expected to attend classes M–W during didactic, but it was hard to plan for the discussion when we did not know until late the day before. I am sure by this time next year it will be much easier to care plan, but it currently takes me a long time as I build my understanding of anesthesia. I also would have appreciated more literature/content in the modules for me to draw from when building my care plans, as it seemed to have stopped after the first few weeks.As far as peer lectures go, I would have appreciated their slides to review while listening to lecture, I found it hard to take notes without it.", "I enjoyed the weekly zoom meetings where students were able to discuss cases and hear their professors input.", "It was helpful having Zooms with Dr. Lorette to discuss the case studies during times when we did not have in person lab."]

comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["Dr. Lorette is a fantastic instructor and an anesthesia expert. Practicing new skills is obviously paramount. Sim was a great way to introduce outside distractions too like having a surgeon and circulator.", ".", "Dr. Lorette supports a positive learning environment, both in class and in the sim lab.", "Dr. Lorette is one of the smartest people I have ever met, and I strive to align my practice after her.", "Dr. Lorette brought enthusiasm and energy to her instruction.", "Knowledgeable, passionate, and fair.", "Dr. Lorette delivers extremely effective lectures for my learning style. There are many ways to explain the content for this course, but the way she articulates it sticks with me.", "I loved the SIM and i liked that we had a few of them so we could get more and more comfortable. I likeds that we got to do care plans as well so we had a good idea on what to think about for them before clinical", "Dr. Lorette is a very knowledgeable clinician, and the way she teaches tends to click with me.  She is also great at playing the scary surgeon.", "Dr. Lorette is always exciting to learn from and I really enjoy listening to her clinical expertise", "I loved this course! I enjoyed being able to practice in SIM and develop my skills and confidence throughout the semester. All of the professors were always willing to provide feedback and support during simulations. I loved how I was able to go over my anesthesia plans for various cases throughout the semester and incorporate them into my SIMs.", "Dr. Lorette did a great job playing the preceptor role and was very supportive throughout the induction simulation.", "The instructors always made themselves available outside of class time. The instructors created a supportive environment and offered feedback and encouragement after every simulation. I liked when we discussed the summer cases that we talked about a variety of different ways anesthesia could be administered to the patient and that there is no one correct way.", "Very knowledgeable and approachable", "Very knowledgeable and enthusiastic about the class. Impressive simulation acting skills."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Connie Lorette)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["Letting students be more hand on in lab/sim", "Specifically, and only because Dr lorette was the one teaching via zoom on sim weeks, just a little bit more heads up on when to expect a zoom class. So i understand the steps to intubating but i dont feel i have a sturdy foundation", "I think just more of a heads up before the zooms would be super helpful to come to class prepared for a meaningful discussion.", "I wish we did a few more SIMs throughout the semester to help me develop more confidence prior to starting clinicals.", "I think it would have been helpful to have open lab or open sim for us to be able to come in and practice on our own time ... especially getting more hands on time with the ventilator!", "more time to practice skills using equipment such as anesthesia machine outside of simulation"]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Janet Dewan)
comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["This is a great course. I love simulation and the opportunity to learn from these professors. However, there was more than once occasions where we got less than 24 hours' notice that we were expected to attend a Zoom class. I think these easily could have been planned out at the beginning of the semester. I know I have heard professors say that we should expect to have school commitments Monday–Wednesday, but it makes it challenging if I have a meeting for my DNP project planned during that time, or something else related to school.", "poor communication, for example, the peer quiz had no information on what would be on it or when the expected time was to take and submit, assignments are graded without even having a rubric for clear expectations on what should be included in the submission", "The course schedule was a little difficult to navigate.", "Went smoothly", ".", "This course was sometimes confusing in its structure....lectures with Dr. Lorette were sometimes announced with very little notice, and expectations were unclear in terms of knowledge level.", "Organization of online content for the course was difficult to navigate at times.", "This course was mostly in–person", "This course's online component was very helpful in preparing me for in person simulation.", "Every instructor was great throughout this course. I think there are a few organizational aspects that could be improved, such as the TBA class Zooms and the TBA peer quiz. I found it difficult to adequately prepare for class when the additional Zoom classes were announced only the day before. This made it challenging to schedule our personal and school commitments without knowing when and what time the extra Zooms would be more than 24 hours in advance. I also could have prepared better for the peer quiz if a date had been specified for its release, but it was opened at the last minute with little prior notice.Regarding SIM, it was nice to finally practice what we've discussed all summer. I think having some form of \"open lab hours\" outside of our scheduled SIM time would be helpful to familiarize ourselves more with the machine and go through the induction/emergence sequence. I also felt quite flustered during the final practice SIM with the addition of the surgeon, especially when they were asking for things while I was laser–focused on completing the anesthesia sequence in the correct order. I understand that this might be similar to real life, but perhaps it would be beneficial to introduce the surgeon and related aspects earlier in the SIMs. Overall, I learned a lot from every professor this semester, and I am excited about the amount I still have to learn.", "I found this class's format challenging. I did not have issues with the peer lectures, as we were notified via the syllabus. But we often did not know if we were meeting for mini zooms with Dr. Lorette until less than 24 hours before. I understand we are expected to attend classes M–W during didactic, but it was hard to plan for the discussion when we did not know until late the day before. I am sure by this time next year it will be much easier to care plan, but it currently takes me a long time as I build my understanding of anesthesia. I also would have appreciated more literature/content in the modules for me to draw from when building my care plans, as it seemed to have stopped after the first few weeks.As far as peer lectures go, I would have appreciated their slides to review while listening to lecture, I found it hard to take notes without it.", "I enjoyed the weekly zoom meetings where students were able to discuss cases and hear their professors input.", "It was helpful having Zooms with Dr. Lorette to discuss the case studies during times when we did not have in person lab."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Janet Dewan)
comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["I have so much respect for Dr. Dewan. I love how she challenges our ways of thinking, and it determined to make us strong clinicians.", "simulation was my favorite part of this course. Every single professor and staff member made the experience as close to real as possible, and really helped me feel like I could step into the shoes of an SRNA. I appreciated the honest feedback for each sim, it was a really valuable learning experience.", "This course was excellent, it was great to apply the knowledge we’ve been learning and start to get a feel for what to expect in clinical as a complete novice. Practicing new skills is obviously paramount. Sim was a great way to introduce outside distractions too like having a surgeon and circulator.", ".", "Dr. Dewan supports a positive learning environment, both in class and in the sim lab. She wants us to learn and do well, that is evident.", "Dr. Dewan is one of the smartest people I have ever met, and is extremely knowledgeable answering any questions we might ask.", "Dr. Dewan demonstrated a great depth of understanding in the topics of the course. A longitudinal perspective of the content, informed by her many years in practice, was both unique and interesting.", "She is very passionate about Sim and makes challenging skills easier to understand.", "Dr. Dewan is extremely knowledgable about the ins and outs of anesthesia. She has a way of sifting through content and highlighting what is most clinically significant. She gave great feedback after the simulations.", "Dr. Dewan did an amazing job at setting up the flow of the lectures leading into sim. She's also an excellent actress.", "Dr. Dewan is great to learn from and makes students feel comfortable during SIM", "The instructors always made themselves available outside of class time. The instructors created a supportive environment and offered feedback and encouragement after every simulation. I liked when we discussed the summer cases that we talked about a variety of different ways anesthesia could be administered to the patient and that there is no one correct way.", "Very intelligent with great insight", "Very knowledgeable, great organization of the class and simulations."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Janet Dewan)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["Only because Dr Dewan and Dr hall were the instructors on the airway training day, i think the airway management class could use some restructure. We had senior students showing us how to do the skills but they admittedly had a hard time explaining some things or teaching airway adjuncts that they’ve either never used themselves or have only used once before. They were also a little timid on correcting mistakes of the juniors and they had a hard time explaining how to DL vs the students who could atleast show what to look for with VL which i found to be a better teaching tool. The teachers were available for questions but it was just as hard for me to ask the teacher for clarification without it coming off as undermining or embarrassing the students who were trying to teach it. The seniors were good at laryngoscopy but not good at explaining and teaching it to novices in my opinion and that made for an awkward dynamic", ".", "The peer teaching quiz was a mystery until late, so maybe having that earlier in the semester and going over what content will be on it.", "It would be great to have more simulations and more opportunities to practice skills.", "Give more time to practice using the equipment and doing the sequences before having to do them live in the simulation lab", "If there were a way for the syllabus to include all of the lectures for this course, that would help with student expectations for when we are expected to be free for class time.", "I felt like I did not have much time practicing skills as I thought I would have. I think the scheduling the AM groups for 2.5 hours and the afternoon group for 1.5 hours put those students at a disadvantage. I realize it was less people, but I still felt like it wasnt enough time, and the environment didnt feel permissive to stay and practice after faculty left.", "I think it would have been helpful to have open lab or open sim for us to be able to come in and practice on our own time ... especially getting more hands on time with the ventilator!", "more time to practice skills using equipment such as anesthesia machine outside of simulation"]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Janet Dewan)
comment_prompt: Please expand on the instructor’s strengths and/or areas for improvement in facilitating inclusive learning.
comments_json: ["I would have loved to have had more lab time, especially airway lab and maybe even more time with the ultrasound –– I think it would have made me feel a little more comfortable going into the clinical setting.", "Strengths: Dr Dewan is extremely intelligent who it seems like has seen just about everything you could think of with anesthesia and she’s great at incorporating the science to explain why we do what we do as anesthesia providersImprovement: none", ".", "I greatly appreciate Dr. Dewan's experience in Anesthesia, and her passion for global healthcare.  She is truly amazing to learn from, and I am lucky to have her as an expert mentor for my project.", "Dr. Dewan is always inclusive of different students' learning styles.", "I felt the final sims were easier/smoother than the first full sim, and maybe that was by design but I found it incredibly stressful to try to get basic skills /flow down while things were going wrong during it. I also think an open skills lab would have been nice to become more familiar with the airway skills and anesthesia machine.", "It was helpful getting to watch our peers perform their simulation. Getting to meet with clinical faculty during last simulation was extremely helpful and eased nerves prior to starting clinical."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Susan Hall)
comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["This is a great course. I love simulation and the opportunity to learn from these professors. However, there was more than once occasions where we got less than 24 hours' notice that we were expected to attend a Zoom class. I think these easily could have been planned out at the beginning of the semester. I know I have heard professors say that we should expect to have school commitments Monday–Wednesday, but it makes it challenging if I have a meeting for my DNP project planned during that time, or something else related to school.", "poor communication, for example, the peer quiz had no information on what would be on it or when the expected time was to take and submit, assignments are graded without even having a rubric for clear expectations on what should be included in the submission", "The course schedule was a little difficult to navigate.", "Went smoothly", ".", "This course was sometimes confusing in its structure....lectures with Dr. Lorette were sometimes announced with very little notice, and expectations were unclear in terms of knowledge level.", "Organization of online content for the course was difficult to navigate at times.", "This course was mostly in–person", "This course's online component was very helpful in preparing me for in person simulation.", "Every instructor was great throughout this course. I think there are a few organizational aspects that could be improved, such as the TBA class Zooms and the TBA peer quiz. I found it difficult to adequately prepare for class when the additional Zoom classes were announced only the day before. This made it challenging to schedule our personal and school commitments without knowing when and what time the extra Zooms would be more than 24 hours in advance. I also could have prepared better for the peer quiz if a date had been specified for its release, but it was opened at the last minute with little prior notice.Regarding SIM, it was nice to finally practice what we've discussed all summer. I think having some form of \"open lab hours\" outside of our scheduled SIM time would be helpful to familiarize ourselves more with the machine and go through the induction/emergence sequence. I also felt quite flustered during the final practice SIM with the addition of the surgeon, especially when they were asking for things while I was laser–focused on completing the anesthesia sequence in the correct order. I understand that this might be similar to real life, but perhaps it would be beneficial to introduce the surgeon and related aspects earlier in the SIMs. Overall, I learned a lot from every professor this semester, and I am excited about the amount I still have to learn.", "I found this class's format challenging. I did not have issues with the peer lectures, as we were notified via the syllabus. But we often did not know if we were meeting for mini zooms with Dr. Lorette until less than 24 hours before. I understand we are expected to attend classes M–W during didactic, but it was hard to plan for the discussion when we did not know until late the day before. I am sure by this time next year it will be much easier to care plan, but it currently takes me a long time as I build my understanding of anesthesia. I also would have appreciated more literature/content in the modules for me to draw from when building my care plans, as it seemed to have stopped after the first few weeks.As far as peer lectures go, I would have appreciated their slides to review while listening to lecture, I found it hard to take notes without it.", "I enjoyed the weekly zoom meetings where students were able to discuss cases and hear their professors input.", "It was helpful having Zooms with Dr. Lorette to discuss the case studies during times when we did not have in person lab."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Susan Hall)
comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["Dr. Hall is amazing. Practicing new skills is obviously paramount. Sim was a great way to introduce outside distractions too like having a surgeon and circulator.", ".", "Dr. Hall supports a positive learning environment, both in class and in the sim lab.", "I never realized how nice Dr. Hall was until this semester, and she genuinely wants us all to excel.  I have emailed her several times about questions and how much I have learned this semester, and I truly mean it.", "She is warm and attentive and clearly wants us all to succeed. As a preceptor during sim, she made me more confident in the simulated OR.", "Dr. Hall is very supportive of her students and is always available to provide clarification on content.", "Dr. Hall was great at letting you know what to expect going in, and making sure you felt comfortable with your plan.", "Dr. Hall makes students feel comfortable and is a great professor to learn from. She is a calming presence as a preceptor during SIM.", "Dr. Hall is very supportive and always does a good job with debriefing.", "The instructors always made themselves available outside of class time. I liked when we discussed the summer cases that we talked about a variety of different ways anesthesia could be administered to the patient and that there is no one correct way.", "Made simulation less daunting", "Very friendly and approachable, made simulation less anxiety inducing."]

course_info: - TRACE report for NRSG7530-01 Nurse Anesthesia Practicum 1  (Susan Hall)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["Only because Dr Dewan and Dr hall were the instructors on the airway training day, i think the airway management class could use some restructure. We had senior students showing us how to do the skills but they admittedly had a hard time explaining some things or teaching airway adjuncts that they’ve either never used themselves or have only used once before. They were also a little timid on correcting mistakes of the juniors and they had a hard time explaining how to DL vs the students who could atleast show what to look for with VL which i found to be a better teaching tool. The teachers were available for questions but it was just as hard for me to ask the teacher for clarification without it coming off as undermining or embarrassing the students who were trying to teach it. The seniors were good at laryngoscopy but not good at explaining and teaching it to novices in my opinion and that made for an awkward dynamic.", ".", "Closer alignment of 7520/7530 syllabuses.", "Nothing", "Give more time to practice using the equipment and doing the sequences before having to do them live in the simulation lab", "N/A", "It might be intentional, but Dr. Hall's speaking cadence and inflection when responding to me tended to make me unsure about what she thought about my intentions/thoughts. I just found her hard to read, and tended to think maybe I was doing something wrong or wasnt doing something I should be becasue she hides emotion well in her features, and the slightest upward inflection at the end of her sentences that I translated into a question mark. I dont think it is ill intentioned at all, and she is often very reassuring, but I found it confusing in the sim setting.See above regarding skills practice.", "I think it would have been helpful to have open lab or open sim for us to be able to come in and practice on our own time ... especially getting more hands on time with the ventilator!", "more time to practice skills using equipment such as anesthesia machine outside of simulation"]

Even though Dr.Nolan will never ask us to memorize the outcome from a research paper, I personally just find it sometimes confusing with all the research.", "Dr. Nolan is funny and a great lecturer, but lectures are confusing and just goes through articles but the articles sometimes contradict each other and I never knew what I needed to know versus not know for exams. However, I appreciate going through CPGs to stay up to date on the most relevant treatments.Please allow the text box setting on ExamSoft.", "Please review quizzes and exams to the whole class, the whole point of a quiz or test is to be a learning opportunity, so it doesn't make sense to me why we don't review and learn from our mistakes. We should be allowed to have scrap paper for exams, especially considering how many charts and graphs there were in the final material.  Please include less research studies, and more just summarize and give the gists. It's hard for me to piece out what I need to know. It's hard to take away points when he talks about contradicting studies, so it's hard to know what we should be doing in practice. I get that there is some ambiguity.", "Maybe a few less slides on the literature, I did find it difficult to study the takeaways when there were so many study based notes in the powerpoint.", "I would have preferred if we had done practice questions or cases during class time to talk through. I feel like all of our lectures/classtime for the second half of the semester were very long and it made it harder to focus or connect to the material. Sometimes I also struggled to know what I needed to pull out/what was important from the research that was presented in the lectures.", "Only thing I could say is to give us a stretch break mid class!! The classes are so long so we tend to trail off by the end. Also informing of us of when we have online lectures would be nice in terms of planning coursework in advance. There was a day that we had off for a holiday and he gave 5 hours worth of lectures to watch (between LE and pelvic/pregnancy content) for a 1.5 hr time slot that we missed.", "Including a few practice questions could be helpful", "too many additional videos to watch outside of class"]

course_info: - TRACE report for PT6405-01 Musculoskeletal Management II  (David Nolan)
comment_prompt: Please expand on the instructor’s strengths and/or areas for improvement in facilitating inclusive learning.
comments_json: ["Can we take a moment to appreciate how direct and clear Dave gives his lectures. Now, the Dave Nolan school of PT is not accredited, but it should be. Dr. Nolan is approachable and kind, and he works hard to include relevant research and stories into his lectures. Can we also appreciate how he gives clinical pearls and talks about mistakes he's made as a PT, so we do not repeat them. Finally, can we appreciate Dr. Nolan. This content from Dr. Nolan has given be a deeper appreciation, truly.", "I appreciate all of the knowledge that Dr. Nolan shares with us, including things that he did wrong or disregarded as a young clinician so that we wouldn't make the same mistake. I did find the length of the powerpoints to be a bit overwhelming, especially when I was going back to study them. I think that a few slides on the literature and evidence based practice is nice, but I wish there was a summarized version about the takeaways from the literature."]

course_info: - TRACE report for PT6406-01 Lab for PT 6405  (Luke Brisbin)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["I felt like the second half of the material on hip, knee, and ankle/foot was somewhat rushed, so it feels like for the amount of material across the 3 joints, there hasn't been as much time to review or as many cases as we did during MSK 1 labs. I also feel like in MSK 1 we reviewed most of, if not all of the diagnoses that we could get/see in a clinic in the future and for practicals, but I don't feel like we've done that as much with the LE. We also didn't have a lab or half a lab dedicated to just practicing cases and treatments right before practical for hip, knee, and ankle like we did in MSK 1 lab and I found those very helpful to feel ready for the practical."]
